title: fix: track response status when flushing
body:
# Description

Fixes a tiny responsewriter gotcha: `Flush()` commits `200 OK` on the real writer, but the wrapper still said status `0` / not written. Streaming handlers can hit this, so metrics/logging may read the wrong thing after the handler returns.

Repro, no magic:
1. Run `go test ./pkg/responsewriter -run TestResponseWriterFlushWritesStatusOK -count=1` on the old code.
2. It fails with `expected: 200, actual: 0`.
3. With this patch, `Flush()` records `200 OK` first, then flushes.

No linked issue found in `gh` search (`responsewriter Flush status`, `status 0 http metrics`, `SSE metrics status`).

## Issue reference

N/A
